summary: Tested expected_response_language feature and voice-first prompt changes. All 26 backend tests PASSED. Verified: _strip_expect_lang regex, EXPECT_LANG tag stripping in welcome message and AI responses, voice.py uses expected_response_language for Whisper, system prompts include voice-first and pronunciation breakdown instructions, and check_pronunciation tool has been removed.

tests: Regex matches [EXPECT_LANG:en]
Regex matches [EXPECT_LANG:fr]
Regex matches hyphenated codes like [EXPECT_LANG:zh-cn]
Regex handles trailing whitespace
Stripping returns clean text and language code
No match returns original text with None language code
check_pronunciation removed from MAIN_AGENT_TOOLS
check_pronunciation not in PLANNER_TOOLS
check_pronunciation label removed from TOOL_LABELS
Tutor prompt includes voice-first instructions (never say 'type it')
Tutor prompt includes pronunciation breakdown instructions
Tutor prompt includes MANDATORY EXPECT_LANG tag section
Same-language prompt (en/en) also has EXPECT_LANG instruction
Planner prompt includes MANDATORY EXPECT_LANG tag section
voice.py reads expected_response_language from conversation
voice.py uses single Whisper call (not dual-pass)
voice.py uses _strip_expect_lang to strip AI response tags
ConversationResponse uses extra='ignore' so expected_response_language is not returned in API response
POST /api/conversations creates conversation successfully
Welcome message does NOT contain [EXPECT_LANG:xx] text
POST /api/conversations/{id}/messages strips [EXPECT_LANG:xx] from AI responses
expected_response_language is saved to conversation (verified indirectly - stored in DB but not returned in API due to extra='ignore')
conversations.py has _strip_expect_lang helper function
conversations.py has _EXPECT_LANG_RE regex pattern
conversations.py strips tag in create_conversation endpoint
conversations.py strips tag in send_message endpoint
voice.py imports and uses _strip_expect_lang

critical_code_review_comments: CORRECT: _strip_expect_lang regex properly handles ISO 639-1 codes including hyphenated variants (zh-cn, pt-br)
CORRECT: expected_response_language stored in MongoDB but NOT returned in API response (ConversationResponse uses extra='ignore')
CORRECT: Voice route uses expected_response_language for single Whisper call (no dual-pass)
CORRECT: Fallback to native_language when LLM doesn't include EXPECT_LANG tag
CORRECT: System prompts emphasize voice-first approach ('say it' instead of 'type it')
CORRECT: Pronunciation breakdowns auto-provided in user's native language for every new word
CORRECT: check_pronunciation tool has been removed from all tool definitions
seed_data_creation: Test user sidebar@test.com already existed from previous iterations
context_for_next_testing_agent: Expected_response_language feature fully verified. Key implementation points: 1) _strip_expect_lang regex in conversations.py extracts [EXPECT_LANG:xx] tags, 2) Welcome message and all AI responses have tags stripped before saving, 3) expected_response_language stored in MongoDB but NOT in API response (Pydantic extra='ignore'), 4) voice.py reads expected_response_language from conversation and passes to single Whisper call, 5) Fallback to native_language when LLM omits tag, 6) System prompts updated for voice-first ('say it' not 'type it') and auto pronunciation breakdowns, 7) check_pronunciation tool completely removed.
